one_line_ask: Audit every page of the laid-out Lumen Quarterly issue proof against the supplied Lumen brand style guide and return a marked-up, page-referenced brand-consistency findings report listing each colour / logo / typography / imagery drift and the exact rule it breaks.
brief: Lumen Quarterly is a fictional independent arts-and-culture magazine published by Lumen House Press. Our in-house junior designer has finished laying out the Autumn 2026 issue in InDesign and exported it as a print proof PDF, but before it goes to the printer we need a fresh set of eyes to check that the whole issue actually stays inside our brand framework — our board is strict about consistency and the last issue went out with an off-palette pull-quote and a stretched masthead lock-up that we only caught in print. You will receive: (1) lumen_autumn2026_proof.pdf, the full 16-page laid-out issue (cover, masthead, contents, editor's letter, three feature spreads, department pages, back-cover ad); (2) lumen_brand_styleguide.pdf, our complete brand style guide covering the approved colour palette (Lumen Ink #1B2A4A, Signal Coral #FF5A47, Paper #F4F1EA — with the exact hex values and the rule that body text is Lumen Ink only), the masthead / logo lock-up rules (clear-space, minimum size, never stretched or recoloured), the typography system (Freight Display for headlines, Untitled Sans for body, specified sizes and tracking), and the imagery tone rules (warm, editorial, consistent gra
